text: Gastrectomy for gastric cancer has been traditionally performed in an open fashion, although currently the laparoscopic approach is commonly used. We present the technique for robotic gastrectomy through a clinical case in a patient with early stage gastric adenocarcinoma. The steps of the procedure will be clearly defined, with an emphasis on surgical technique and utilization of instruments. The visualization achieved with the robotic approach is unparalleled, particularly regarding dissection and division of vessels. A modified D-II lymphadenectomy will be depicted, and one can appreciate the precision afforded by use of the robot.

The robotic surgery platform offers technical advantages over the laparoscopic approach for gastrectomy for cancer, and it's been shown to result in decreased blood loss, equivalent lymphadenectomy, and equivalent perioperative morbidity. Robotic gastrectomy is safe and feasible in selected patients.
